title: SDLC Workflow Artifact Frontmatter
description: JSON Schema for the YAML frontmatter of every artifact produced by the sdlc-workflow plugin. Each artifact is a markdown file under .ai/workflows/<slug>/ (or .ai/simplify/, .ai/profiles/ for off-pipeline outputs). The frontmatter block (between the leading `---` markers) is parsed as YAML and validated against the matching branch of `oneOf` selected by the `type` field.

iso8601.description: ISO-8601 timestamp. Templates emit values like '2026-05-11T14:30:00Z'.

runId.description: Compact timestamp run-id used for ship runs, simplify runs, and profile runs.

slug.description: kebab-case workflow slug. Must match the parent directory name.

revisionLedger.description: Reason-centric revision ledger written by the additive-write contract (single source: skills/wf/reference/_additive-write.md). One entry per re-invocation that actually changed the artifact; the body is a living document (rewritten in place), the ledger records WHY each revision happened. Distinct from the on-disk history/ snapshots, which hold verbatim prior wording. Replaced the old in-body `## Revision N` append pattern in v9.105.0.

base.description: Fields shared by every workflow artifact.

regenerable.description: When true, the renderer treats this artifact as a view over other state. Sub-commands may overwrite freely; the calm-reader surfaces a `regenerable` badge. Added in v9.20.0 as part of the additive-write contract.

provenance.description: How the artifact's content was produced. Absent or `authored` = written forward by its stage (the default; do not stamp it explicitly). `adopted` = reverse-engineered from an already-existing diff by `/wf intake adopt`. Downstream stages and any pattern-mining over 04-plan/05-implement (retro learnings, reuse analysis) MUST treat `adopted` artifacts as reconstruction, not forethought. Added in v9.118.0.

steering-honored.description: What standing-steering entries (from the workflow's user-owned `steer.md`) this stage honored, e.g. ["avoided config/loader.ts", "used the queue approach"]. Present only when `steer.md` exists; omit the field entirely otherwise. See `reference/_steering.md` (the W6 standing-steering contract). Added in v9.120.0.

refs.description: Cross-references between artifacts. Free-form: keys are role names, values are filenames or arrays of filenames.

stage-status.description: Human-readable progress label for the current stage (e.g. 'in-progress', 'blocked', 'awaiting-review'). Written by stage reference files; surfaced by session-start hook.

selected-slice-or-focus.description: Alias for selected-slice used by stage reference files and the session-start hook when a focus target (rather than a formal slice) is in play. Preferred over selected-slice by skill references; the hook falls back to selected-slice when this is absent.

recommended-next-command.description: Preferred-alias next-command written by some stage references and the hotfix/resume skills. The session-start hook reads this first with next-command as fallback.

recommended-next-invocation.description: Preferred-alias next-invocation written by some stage references and the hotfix/resume skills. The session-start hook reads this first with next-invocation as fallback.

recommended-next-stage.description: Optional stage recommendation written by stage reference files to guide the next /wf invocation.

runtime-evidence-deferrals.description: Open/cleared runtime-evidence deferrals (RUNTIME-PROBE-PLAN §2.4). /wf ship HARD-BLOCKS while any entry has cleared-by: null. cleared-by must be an EVIDENCE descriptor (a probe/re-verify run that produced matching runtime evidence), never a prose risk-acceptance string — PO risk-acceptance uses ship-override-authorization instead.

cleared-by.description: null until truly evidenced; set to a probe/evidence descriptor when a /wf probe (or re-verify) run produces matching runtime evidence. NOT a place for risk-acceptance prose.

cleared-acs.description: For a multi-AC deferral, the subset of AC ids evidenced so far (partial progress) while cleared-by stays null.

absorbed-by.description: Slices that inherited this deferral (INTENT-FIDELITY W5.3). Absorbing the same deferral into a THIRD slice is a stop, not an absorption — verify surfaces it as an inheritance-breadth decision (provision the clearing event now, or PO-accept explicitly).

needed-by.description: The slice by which this deferral's clearing evidence is due (INTENT-FIDELITY W9.2, set at plan time). status escalates when the needed-by slice is complete while cleared-by is still null; yolo stops on a charter-critical prerequisite past its needed-by boundary.

probe.description: The literal capability-probe command + one-line output tail executed the run this deferral was written (YOLO F1/F2 attempt-before-declare). Carried into the ship-block hand-back so /wf ship shows receipted deferrals.

ship-override-authorization.description: Explicit PO risk-acceptance that lets ship proceed past an un-evidenced deferral (e.g. deploy-time-circular config). Distinct from cleared-by: it is an OVERRIDE surfaced in the ship summary, not evidence.

charter.description: Charter — the intake's 3-7 POSITIVE commitments as first-class spec (INTENT-FIDELITY W8.1). Deliberately few; a charter that restates the whole intake is boilerplate. Commitments must be falsifiable by code. RIM adjudications name the charter ids they protect; the intent-fidelity dimension checks question 1 per-id; the charter scenario (shape) exercises the core loop end-to-end. Compressed lifecycles (fix/hotfix/refactor/update-deps/adopt) carry no charter.

commitment.description: One falsifiable positive commitment, e.g. 'the model decides whether and what to probe in the interview'.

source.description: Where in the intake it was committed, e.g. 01-intake.md#restated-request step 2.

status.description: Set by verify runs + the intent-fidelity review + yolo checkpoints. A broken commitment is a yolo stop.

evidence-quality.description: Slug-level evidence-rung rollup (INTENT-FIDELITY W9.1 + YOLO F5). Counts of user-observable AC evidence by highest rung, aggregated from the 06-verify AC status tables. The dashboard renders it as a chip; a slug with zero live-rung evidence on charter-cited ACs cannot ship without recorded PO acceptance.

unproven-integrations.description: External integrations whose real behaviour has not been observed live in this workflow (INTENT-FIDELITY W5.2). While first-light is null, ACs depending on the integration cap at partial (mock/emulator rungs are proxies, never pass). Any live observation (tagged smoke, /wf probe, live e2e) stamps first-light.

introduced-by.description: The slice that introduced the integration.

first-light.description: ISO-8601 of first live observation; null until observed. Set by verify.

intent-risks.description: Intent-Risk (RIM) ledger (INTENT-FIDELITY W1). Intake distils the 'Risks if Misunderstood' prose into tracked entries; shape MUST adjudicate every open one (set status + decision, PO-ratify when it touches a PO directive) before it can complete; handoff/ship HARD-BLOCK on any status: open, and surface carried ones. Mirrors the runtime-evidence-deferrals machinery.

risk.description: One-line statement of the intake risk.

status.description: open = not yet decided (illegal after shape completes); adjudicated = shape named the choice; carried = genuinely cannot resolve at shape, receiving stage named in Open Questions.

adjudicated-by.description: Artifact + section that adjudicated it, e.g. 02-shape.md#desired-behavior. Empty until adjudicated.

decision.description: One line: what was chosen and why (the tradeoff). Restating the risk without a decision is illegal.

po-ratified.description: true | false | 'not-required' | null (unset). When the risk touches a PO directive, must be true (cite the po-answers.md entry) or an explicit PO-declined false; not-required only when the decision alters no PO directive.

designContractFrontmatter.description: 02c-craft.md — written by /wf design craft as the implementation-facing visual contract.

metric-acceptance-mock-rung.description: Count of USER-OBSERVABLE AC whose highest evidence-rung is cited-mock / uncited-mock / static (the machine-readable projection of the per-AC evidence-rung labels in the ## Acceptance Criteria Status body). The post-write-verify mockEvidenceGate HARD-BLOCKS result: pass while this is > 0 (INTENT-FIDELITY W5.2/W9.1 + YOLO F5). Absent/0 on a slice with no user-observable-mock AC.

skipped-gating-specs.description: Specs designated as the evidence for a specific AC that were SKIPPED (guard exit, .skip/.todo, missing env/secret, filtered out) while the rest of the suite ran (YOLO F4). A skipped gating spec is a missing-evidence event for its AC — that AC cannot inherit the suite's green. Empty when no skipped spec gates an AC.

precondition.description: The unmet precondition, e.g. 'set BETTER_AUTH_SECRET and re-run'.

convergence.description: Whether verify results converged, escalated, or were not applicable. Written by /wf verify to 06-verify-<slice>.md.

interactive-verification.description: Set by /wf verify. `deferred` means at least one user-observable AC could not be evidenced in this environment; the result MUST then be `partial` (never `pass`) and a matching 00-index runtime-evidence-deferrals entry is required. The post-write-verify result gate blocks `result: pass` while this is `deferred`.

interactive-verification-defer-reason.description: Required when interactive-verification == deferred. Must enumerate the constraint-resolution-ladder rungs tried and the residual that survives them — not a bare 'no device'.

not.$comment: Forbids the invented `metric-acceptance-unverified-interactive` field. The only sanctioned way to record an unverified user-observable AC is `interactive-verification: deferred` + a 00-index runtime-evidence-deferrals entry (see post-write-verify result gate and the AC-VERIFIABILITY recommendations R7).

runs.description: Compact per-invocation audit trail for the accumulating review ledger. One entry per `/wf review` run; rendered invisibly (frontmatter only). Lets retro/handoff/auto reconstruct how many passes a review took without a body-level round section.

handoff-scope.description: How this handoff was invoked. `slug` = the classic per-slug handoff. `branch` = a branch/PR-scoped batch run that aggregates every slug whose 00-index.md shares this branch (v9.105.0). Absent → treat as `slug`.

handoff-fingerprint.description: No-op guard for the additive-write contract: a stable hash of the inputs this handoff was packaged from (commit range + slice statuses + review verdicts). A re-run whose recomputed fingerprint matches this value changed nothing and is skipped (no snapshot, no ledger entry).

handoff-lead.description: In a branch-scoped batch handoff, the single slug that OWNS the branch-level PR-readiness block (CI watch, triage, rebase, verdict). Recorded on first batch run and stable across re-runs. On the lead's own artifact, equals its slug.

readiness-via.description: Follower-slug pointer to the lead slug's handoff artifact that carries the authoritative branch-level readiness block (e.g. `<lead-slug>/08-handoff.md`). Present only on non-lead slugs in a batch handoff; when set, this artifact's own readiness fields are advisory and the PR-level verdict lives on the lead.

branch-slugs.description: Every workflow slug whose 00-index.md shares this branch and was in scope for the batch handoff (the roster). Present only when handoff-scope is `branch`.

pr-readiness-verdict.description: Branch/PR-level readiness = AND across every slug on the branch. Distinct from the per-slug `readiness-verdict`: a slug can be individually ready while the PR is not (a sibling slug's commits are unreviewed). Ship gates on this, not on the per-slug field. Written on the lead slug.

ship-plan-readiness.description: Verdict of the shared ship-plan readiness pre-check (handoff step 6.7 / ship step 0.4, in _ship-plan-readiness.md). `ok` = plan present + drift-clean; `acknowledged` = drift found but the user recorded an explicit per-run reason to proceed; `not-applicable` = handoff only, shipping handled outside /wf ship. `missing`/`drift` cause a STOP (handoff writes status: awaiting-input), so a completed artifact never carries them. Absent on artifacts predating the pre-check → treat as `skipped`.

Verdict of the shared ship-plan readiness pre-check at ship step 0.4 (_ship-plan-readiness.md). A written ship run only ever carries `ok` or `acknowledged` — a `missing`/`drift`/cancel outcome STOPs before the 13-step sequence, so no run artifact is written. Absent on runs predating the pre-check → treat as `skipped`.

ship-scope.description: How this ship was invoked. `slug` = classic single-slug ship. `branch` = a branch/PR-scoped batch ship where every slug on the branch merges atomically as one run (v9.105.0). Absent → `slug`.

branch-slugs.description: Every workflow slug on the branch that this single ship run released together. Present only when ship-scope is `branch`; the run artifact lives on the lead slug and followers carry a pointer.

shipped-via.description: Follower-slug pointer to the lead slug's ship-run artifact that actually executed the merge/tag/release for this branch (e.g. `<lead-slug>/09-ship-run-<run-id>.md`). Present only on non-lead slugs in a batch ship; their own 00-index advances to shipped without a duplicate run artifact.
For a follower slug in a batch ship: the lead slug's ship-run artifact this row points to. Lets a follower's run index record the release without a duplicate run artifact.

shipRollbackFrontmatter.description: Runbook-driven reversal record (09-rollback-<run-id>.md) written by the rollback phase of /wf ship (reference/ship/rollback.md). run-id names the ship run being REVERSED; the original run gains rolled-back: true + rollback-artifact. Added in v9.100.0 (feedback-loops W4).

solutionFrontmatter.description: Durable cross-workflow learning under .ai/solutions/<category>/<slug>.md, distilled by /wf retro (producer) and read by /wf plan's learnings scan (consumer). Added in v9.100.0 (feedback-loops W1). Body is Problem / Learning / How to apply, <= ~30 lines.

source-workflow.description: Originating workflow slug; becomes a list when later workflows refresh the learning (dedupe-on-write).

recapFrontmatter.description: Plain-language catch-up / explain artifact written by /wf recap (formerly resume). scope: workflow (whole run), slice (one slice), or explain (a focus artifact). focus is the explained artifact when scope=explain.

resumeFrontmatter.description: DEPRECATED alias of recap (renamed 2026-07). Retained one release so a legacy 90-resume.md still validates; new writes use type:recap.

simplifyRunFrontmatter.description: Standalone simplify run. New runs root a type:workflow-index slug workflow and write the lead to .ai/workflows/<slug>/01-simplify.md (carrying a slug extra field; run-id kept for continuity). Legacy runs at .ai/simplify/<run-id>.md still validate + render via the retained off-pipeline discovery.

rcaFrontmatter.description: 01-rca.md — root-cause analysis workflow produced by /wf intake rca.

profileFrontmatter.description: Written to .ai/profiles/<run-id>/01-profile.md — standalone, no workflow slug.

shipLegacyFrontmatter.description: Deprecated 09-ship.md — no current writer emits this. Kept here so the validator does not reject historical files in old workflow dirs.

designAuditFrontmatter.description: 07-design-audit.md — measurable technical quality audit for implemented design.

projectContextFrontmatter.description: PRODUCT.md / DESIGN.md project-level context rendered under .ai/_view/project/.

shipPlanFrontmatter.description: .ai/ship-plan.md project-level shipping contract read by /wf ship and built by /wf-meta build-pipeline. v9.35.0: required[] aligned to the actual init-ship-plan template (project-name/plan-version), dropping the stray title/status/source that were copied from project-context and never emitted. v9.42.0: documented the inbound-half blocks (code-quality / local-dx / governance) as optional objects — the branch stays open (no additionalProperties:false), so these are documentation, not enforcement; inner shapes live in the init-ship-plan reference. v9.43.0: added Block K (security) as a documented object; governance gained merge controls + branch-protection fidelity fields, ship-environments gained per-env protection, ci-pipeline gained ci-ergonomics, plus a repo-topology field — all nested in already-open keys.

code-quality.description: Block H — inbound code-quality gates: format-check / lint / type-check / test-coverage (each {tool,cmd}), commit-convention, pr-title-convention. Read by /wf-meta build-pipeline (Audits K-L).

local-dx.description: Block I — local developer experience: git-hooks (framework + per-hook commands), editorconfig, runtime-version-files, task-runner, bootstrap-cmd, contributing-doc. Read by build-pipeline (Audits M-N).

governance.description: Block J — repo governance: branch-protection (mechanism, code-owner/conversation/linear-history fidelity, apply-via: gh-api|manual), codeowners, pr-template, issue-templates, dependency-automation, and merge controls. Read by build-pipeline (Audits O, R).

security.description: Block K — security & supply-chain gates: sast, dependency-audit, secret-scanning, sbom, license-check. Read by build-pipeline (Audit P). Aligns to skills/review/reference/supply-chain.md.

repo-topology.description: Discovery output (not a block): { monorepo, tool, workspaces[] }. Biases dependency-automation ecosystems, lint-staged scoping, and CI matrix.

announceFrontmatter.description: Release/change announcement. v9.35.0: required[] relaxed to schema/type/slug to match the wf-meta announce template (which emits created-at + audiences/channels lists); the richer fields below stay optional.

ideationFrontmatter.description: Idea-backlog artifact. New runs root a type:workflow-index slug workflow and write the lead to .ai/workflows/<slug>/01-ideate.md (carrying a slug extra field; focus stays the schema key). Legacy runs at .ai/ideation/<focus>-<ts>.md still validate + render via the retained off-pipeline discovery. Ranked candidate list.

quickMetaArtifactFrontmatter.description: Lightweight intake-mode / wf-meta lane artifacts written under .ai/workflows/<slug>/ (e.g. 00-index.md with type workflow-index, 01-fix.md, hf-brief.md, 99-close.md, 90-next.md). Validated for a known type + schema/type/slug presence; richer per-lane fields are renderer-defensive and most fall through to the generic renderer. Registered in v9.35.0 after an audit found these lanes were silently blocked by the pre/post-write hooks. Each type stays uniquely discriminating, so the oneOf remains unambiguous. Tighten into per-type branches if/when their field contracts stabilize.

siblingYamlSchemas.description: Schemas for sibling .yaml files that ride alongside a .md artifact to carry structured display data the renderer's fragment + figure-canvas builders consume. Validated by scripts/verify-fragment.mjs Check 7. Added in v9.20.0. Phase 2 (v9.21.0) added review-dimension for per-dimension review pages. Phase 3 (v9.22.0) added simplify-run, profile, benchmark, experiment, and instrument for off-pipeline simplify, profile, and generic augmentation artifacts.

review-dimension.description: Per-dimension review (07-review/<dimension>.yaml). One card per dimension; the combined 07-review.yaml still aggregates all dimensions, and the renderer cross-links the two. Added in v9.21.0.

status.description: Accumulating-ledger lifecycle: open | deferred | dismissed | fixed | could-not-fix | resolved. Permissive string for back-compat; the merge protocol in review.md owns the vocabulary.

pre-existing.description: true iff the defect exists on the base branch untouched by this workflow's diff (diff test; git blame tiebreaker). Pre-existing findings never count toward the verdict or blocker gate — they surface in the Pre-existing Debt bucket. Added in v9.99.0 (optional, additive).

dimensions.description: Per-dimension roll-up. Accepts the legacy {name,count} shape OR the live {key,status,blocker,high,med,low,nit} per-severity shape.

status.description: Accumulating-ledger lifecycle: open | deferred | dismissed | fixed | could-not-fix | resolved. Permissive string for back-compat; review.md owns the vocabulary.

pre-existing.description: true iff the defect exists on the base branch untouched by this workflow's diff (diff test; git blame tiebreaker). Pre-existing findings never count toward the verdict or blocker gate — they surface in the Pre-existing Debt bucket. Added in v9.99.0 (optional, additive).

five_whys.description: Optional collapsible drill chain rendered below the causal-chain figure. Each step ladders from the surface symptom toward the root cause. Phase 2 (v9.21.0).

modules.description: Module groupings for the file-change topology. Accepts legacy path-prefix strings (files bucket by prefix) OR rich { id, label, role } objects that files reference by id via files[].module.

oneOf.description: Rich module — identified by `id` (preferred) or `name`; files reference it via files[].module. Extra keys (files, kind, new, description) are tolerated.

role.description: File category (config/infra/ui/domain/…) or, in the legacy convention, the change-type. Topology coloring reads `status` first, then falls back to `role`.

status.description: Change-type for topology coloring (new convention). Legacy plans put this in `role`.

module.description: id of the modules[] entry this file belongs to.

delta.description: A total LOC delta (number), a terse string (e.g. "+5/-2"), or a split { add, rem } object.

kind.description: Relationship label — import/replaces/crosses-service plus open-ended kinds authors use (config, api-contract, css-class, contract, …).

lanes.description: Optional swim-lane projection for plans that span services. When present (or when an edge has kind=crosses-service), the renderer emits a data-flow lane figure instead of the per-module file topology. Added in v9.21.0.

design-contract.description: Sibling YAML for design-contract (visual contract) fragments, authored by /wf design craft next to 02c-craft.md. Carries the token/state/size/theme coverage axes plus the per-element implementation contract so design-contract.mjs can build the interactive coverage grid without scraping prose. NOTE: required[] is intentionally minimal — there is no real authored corpus yet (this is the first writer), so design-contract is NOT in SIBLING_YAML_VALIDATED_TYPES; it joins the write-time hard-validate allowlist only once a real sibling proves the shape.

contract.description: Per-element implementation-contract rows the fragment renders as expandable entries.

design-critique.description: Sibling YAML for design critique fragments. Carries row-level findings so renderers can build critique tables without scraping prose.

design-audit.description: Sibling YAML for design audit fragments. Carries severity-graded violations and remediation status for renderer tables.

simplify-run.description: Sibling YAML for off-pipeline simplify runs (.ai/simplify/<run-id>.md). Drives the finding-table fragment introduced in v9.22.0 (Phase 3). Styled like the review fragment but at smaller scale: no verdict block, categorical chips (reuse/quality/efficiency) instead of severity, optional deltas summary.

deltas.description: Optional summary of proposed code deltas (file-level add/rem counts).

profile.description: Sibling YAML for off-pipeline profile/benchmark runs (.ai/profiles/<run-id>/01-profile.md). Drives the benchmark-comparison fragment introduced in v9.22.0 (Phase 3): hotspots table + before/after metric bars + optimization candidates list.

comparisons.description: Before/after metric pairs for the benchmark-comparison figure. When absent the figure is omitted.

benchmark.description: Sibling YAML for augmentation/benchmark artifacts. Drives the augmentation structured-result fragment (v9.22.0).

experiment.description: Sibling YAML for augmentation/experiment artifacts. Drives the augmentation structured-result fragment (v9.22.0).

instrument.description: Sibling YAML for augmentation/instrument artifacts. Drives the augmentation structured-result fragment (v9.22.0).

sync-report.description: Branch-sync health report (00-sync.yaml beside 00-sync.md). A regenerable view over git/PR state: how far the feature branch has diverged from its base and whether a rebase is safe. `conflict_risk` drives the verdict tone; `diverged_files[]` drives both the desktop drift table and the mobile file rows; `recommendation` populates the callout. Added in v9.44.0.
